blockingIssues.description: Out-of-scope behavioral changes not separated or reverted. OAuth runtime sessionStorage layer (app.js:6193-6368), allowWhileOnboarding bypass on ~16 call sites (app.js:4589-4592), and sheet-ID tightening MIN_PLAUSIBLE_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_LENGTH=20 (app.js:229) all remain in commit a5994fc alongside dead-code cleanup. The fix feature only added documentation in architecture.md — it did not separate behavioral changes into their own commits or revert them as the feature description required.

Original fabricated commit ID 'a5994fc6e1c40d3a8e4f5c2b3d7a9e1f2b3c4d5e' from performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup handoff was never formally acknowledged or corrected. Fix handoff uses correct short hash 'a5994fc' but does not address the fabrication.

Tech-debt-auditor droid (.factory/droids/tech-debt-auditor.droid:63-64) contains non-functional ripgrep commands. Both equality regex patterns use look-around assertions ((?<!=)==(?!=) and (?<!.)=(?!=)) that ripgrep does not support without --pcre2 flag, producing 'regex parse error' on execution. Additionally the != pattern (?<!.)=(?!=) is logically incorrect — it matches = not preceded by any character, never matching != in actual code.

Refactoring library references not fixed. .factory/library/refactoring.md still contains 3 issues from prior review: (1) line 187 references non-existent npm run test:contract:dashboard, (2) line 261 references non-existent tests/apps-script-classification.test.mjs, (3) line 81 uses confusing cp-to-/dev/null instead of test -f. The worker handoff incorrectly claimed verification passed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit 'one concern per commit' enforcement with commit-provenance validation: when a worker's handoff cites a commitId, the orchestrator or scrutiny validator must verify it resolves as a real git object before acceptance. Workers must not fabricate commit hashes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup fabricated commit ID. The fix feature (performance-cleanup-out-of-scope-unbundle) used the correct short hash but never formally corrected the fabrication. Round 2 still flags this.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the 'separate or revert' semantics for scrutiny remediation features. If documentation-only remediation is acceptable for bundled changes that already have regression test coverage, codify that explicitly. Otherwise, enforce that behavioral changes must be extracted into separate commits before the fix is considered resolved.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: performance-cleanup-out-of-scope-unbundle interpreted 'separating or reverting' as 'documenting' the bundled changes. 14 regression tests exist across 3 test files but the one-concern-per-commit convention remains violated.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to actually execute any droid/skill commands they modify or create, not just describe expected behavior. Handoff claims of verification must be backed by command output evidence.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: guardrails-nonblocking-scrutiny-followups handoff claims tech-debt-auditor regex was verified but the rg commands fail immediately on execution. The refactoring.md references were also claimed verified but 3 specific issues from the prior review remain.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that ripgrep (rg) does not support look-around assertions (lookahead/lookbehind) without the --pcre2 flag. This is a common gotcha when writing regex patterns for rg commands in droids and skills.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tech-debt-auditor.droid introduced look-around patterns that fail on execution. Both prior round review and this fix feature missed the ripgrep limitation.

rejectedObservations.observation: architecture.md documentation is accurate and useful for the three bundled changes.
rejectedObservations.reason: Positive finding already captured in non-blocking issue of the review report; not actionable as a guidance update.

rejectedObservations.observation: 14 regression tests already cover the three bundled behavioral changes.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is factual context supporting the documentation-only approach, but doesn't resolve the blocking one-concern-per-commit violation. Not a separate guidance update.
